name: Alto de la Cabeza Trail (PRC-BU 77)
name_original: Sendero del Alto de la Cabeza (PRC-BU 77) — circular desde Ciruelos de Cervera por Briongos de Cervera
description: Hike the difficult 12.0-mile Sendero del Alto de la Cabeza (PRC-BU 77) for panoramic views and diverse landscapes in Burgos.
region_intro: Hiking around Ciruelos De Cervera offers access to a landscape characterized by rolling hills, dense juniper forests, and historical sites. The region features varied terrain, including moderate ascents and paths winding through natural parks. Elevations are generally gentle, with routes exploring woodlands and open areas. Best hiking trails around Ciruelos De Cervera The most popular hiking route is Hermitages Trail (PRC-BU 120), a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through historical sites and natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sendero del Sabinar del Arlanza (PR-BU-78) — circular desde Espinosa de Cervera, a moderate 9.2 miles (14.7 km) path. This trail winds through juniper forests and offers panoramic views. Local hikers also love the Sabinar del Arlanza Trail (PR-BU 78), a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail exploring the unique juniper woodlands of the Arlanza region, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Hiking around Ciruelos De Cervera is defined by juniper forests, rolling hills, and historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Ciruelos De Cervera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Nearly 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Ciruelos De Cervera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Ciruelos De Cervera?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes recorded in Ciruelos De Cervera, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find 7 easy routes, 18 moderate trails, and 3 more challenging options.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Ciruelos De Cervera?
A: Yes, Ciruelos De Cervera features several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292500300/sendero-del-sabinar-del-arlanza-pr-bu-78-circular-desde-espinosa-de-cervera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Sabinar del Arlanza (PR-BU-78) — circular desde Espinosa de Cervera&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through juniper forests. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040397338/espinosa-de-cervera-circular-route-through-the-sabinares-de-arlanza-la-yecla-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;circular route through the Sabinares de Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; from Espinosa de Cervera.

Q: What kind of natural sights can I expect to see on hikes in Ciruelos De Cervera?
A: The region is known for its unique juniper forests and rolling hills. Hikers can discover impressive natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6062888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Churrión Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Yecla Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4127823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. There are also viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4997641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Peña de Carazo&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to explore while hiking?
A: Absolutely. Ciruelos De Cervera's trails often lead through areas rich in history. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2521846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Santo Domingo de Silos&lt;/a&gt;, a significant religious building, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery&lt;/a&gt;, famous from cinema.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in Ciruelos De Cervera?
A: Hikes in Ciruelos De Cervera vary in length. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040400800/sabinar-del-arlanza-trail-pr-bu-78" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sabinar del Arlanza Trail (PR-BU 78)&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours 39 minutes, while the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292908981/alto-de-la-cabeza-trail-prc-bu-77" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cabeza Trail (PRC-BU 77)&lt;/a&gt; can take over 5 hours to complete.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Ciruelos De Cervera?
A: The hiking routes in Ciruelos De Cervera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's unique juniper forests, the variety of historical sites, and the well-maintained paths that wind through both woodlands and open areas.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, the region offers routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292908981/alto-de-la-cabeza-trail-prc-bu-77" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cabeza Trail (PRC-BU 77)&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult route covers over 19 km and involves significant elevation gain, leading to rewarding views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5283553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cabeza Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ciruelos De Cervera?
A: Ciruelos De Cervera offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly popular due to milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes ideal, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience, though some higher trails might require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by viewpoints or offer scenic panoramas?
A: Many trails in Ciruelos De Cervera provide excellent scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292500300/sendero-del-sabinar-del-arlanza-pr-bu-78-circular-desde-espinosa-de-cervera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Sabinar del Arlanza (PR-BU-78)&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is known for its panoramic vistas. You can also seek out specific highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4997641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Peña de Carazo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3568119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Peña Alta (1078 m)&lt;/a&gt; for breathtaking landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails?
A: The terrain in Ciruelos De Cervera is varied, characterized by rolling hills, dense juniper forests, and paths winding through natural parks. You'll encounter moderate ascents and descents, with routes exploring both woodlands and open areas. The paths are generally well-defined, suitable for a range of hiking abilities.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options?
A: Yes, Ciruelos De Cervera has 7 easy routes perfect for families or those looking for a relaxed stroll. These trails typically have gentle elevations and shorter distances, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty of the region without significant effort.
tips: Alto de la Cabeza is the highest point in the Ribera del Duero with its 1343 m high. The top is crowned by a metal cross.
Small town located north of Ciruelos de Cervera and which is a smaller town than the one mentioned above. Population that gives entrance to the Natural Park

A small town next to the Sabinares del Arlanza Natural Area. It is worth seeing its church of San Sebastián Mártir from the 13th century, the hermitage of Carmen, an old laundry room and the fountain in the Plaza Mayor.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero del Alto de la Cabeza trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as difficult. It involves an accumulated elevation gain of approximately 380 meters over nearly 19.4 km. A good physical condition and steady footing are required, aligning with CAS (Club Alpino Suizo) levels 2-3.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail officially starts next to the church in Ciruelos de Cervera. You'll find an Information Panel and Trailhead there, which is a good indicator for parking in the village.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The recommended seasons for hiking this trail are Spring, Summer, and Autumn. These seasons generally offer more favorable weather conditions for enjoying the diverse landscapes.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Sendero del Alto de la Cabeza?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite diverse. You'll start among orchards, then descend into the narrow gorge of the "Hoz del arroyo del Berro" with limestone cliffs, holm oaks, and junipers. The path continues through agricultural fields, a poplar grove, and skirts the Valdegatón hill. The highest sections, especially around the Alto de la Cabeza Summit, feature juniper forests and rocky terrain.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Sendero del Alto de la Cabeza trail?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit prohibitions against dogs, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife, including birds of prey, and varying terrain. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the impressive "Hoz del arroyo del Berro" gorge with formations like Cueva de Santiago, Piedra la Yedra, and Risco del Tío Pocho. The Alto de la Cabeza Summit offers stunning panoramic views. You'll also pass the Las Peñas de Cervera Viewpoint and a signposted mirador near Briongos de Cervera with exceptional vistas.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Calle Alta, Pasaje del Lavadero, Calle las Eras, Calle el Palomar, Calle el Portalejo, Calle Carretas, and Calle Molinos, particularly within the villages.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Sabinares de Arlanza-La Yecla Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Sendero del Alto de la Cabeza, as it is a public trail within the Sabinares de Arlanza-La Yecla Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations and signage regarding conservation.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The rocky cliffs and diverse habitats along the trail, especially in the Peñas de Cervera, provide a home for a varied and abundant population of birds of prey. Keep an eye out for them soaring above the limestone formations.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants in Ciruelos de Cervera or Briongos de Cervera?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and passes through Ciruelos de Cervera and Briongos de Cervera. These are small villages, and while specific establishments can change, you may find local cafes or restaurants to refuel before or after your hike. It's always a good idea to check availability beforehand.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing the Sendero del Alto de la Cabeza is around 5 hours, though this can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views or explore points of interest.

waypoints.name: Information Panel and Trailhead, Sabinares de Arlanza-La Yecla Natural Park
